rulings.Case Title: WONG VS THRIFTY CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Further Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Additional Fees;
rulings.Rulings: Plaintiff's Motion for Fees: COURT AWARDS $340,350 AS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES. 1361 HOURS MULTIPLIED BY $250/HOUR. NO MULTIPLIER. NO FURTHER COSTS. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: WONG VS THRIFTY CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Motion To Vacate Clerk'S Judgment Entered Pursuant To Defts' Ccp 998 Offer To Compromise; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Defendant's Renewed Motion to Vacate: DENY because (1) UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 473; (2) FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1008(b)--NO NEW LAW OR FACTS; AND (3) DEFENDANT FAILS ON THE MERITS. See Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc., (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 658; Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc., (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 259-260. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: WONG VS THRIFTY CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Defendant's Motion to Tax: COURT REJECTS BUSSEY REASONING AND GRANTS MOTION TO TAX IN PART. The court strikes (1) $210.50 for transcripts under CCP 1033.5(b)(5); (2) $282.50 for expert witness fees under CCP 1033.5(b)(1); (3) $1,211.80 for photocopying, $95.91 for postage, $12.61 in phone, $78.69 for next day mail, and $121.00 in fax under CCP 1033.5(b)(3); (4) $16.00 in bridge tolls under Ladas v. CSAA, (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 775; (5) $50.20 in computer research under Ladas v. CSAA, (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 776. Under CCP 1033.5(c)(4), the court awards $460.00 in messenger fees. See Ladas v. CSAA, (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 776. Under CRC 26, the court strikes all of the appellate costs except for the $231.38 for production of additional evidence. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: WONG VS THRIFTY CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Fro Code Of Civ.Pro. 128.7 Sanctions Directed Against Defts'A And Attorney Jasinski
rulings.Rulings: Plaintiff's Motion for Sanctions: DENY PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS BECAUSE MOTION MOOT. See Cromwell III v. Cummings, (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th Supp. 10, 13-14; Malovec v. Hamrell, (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 434, 441. DENY DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: RONDA THOMAS VS CMGI,INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FRAUD, 2ND ACTION OF ACTION NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION NEGLIGENCE, 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SHOW FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THEM AS TO THESE DEFENDANTS. MOTION DENIED AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT, AND 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT, AND THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, AS THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. (JH)

rulings.Rulings: DENY WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOTION TO BE MADE BEFORE COMMISSIONER BEST. (PB)
